content.problemStatement:
There are NNN enemies standing from left to right with strengths S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1‚Äã,S2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,SN‚Äã.

MMM bullets are to be fired from the left side such that only one bullet is fired at a time. The ithi^{th}ith bullet (1‚â§i‚â§M)(1 \le i \le M)(1‚â§i‚â§M) has power PiP_iPi‚Äã and capacity CiC_iCi‚Äã.

This means that the ithi^{th}ith bullet affects the first CiC_iCi‚Äã **alive** enemies from the left side and decreases the strength of each of those CiC_iCi‚Äã enemies by PiP_iPi‚Äã.

Once an enemy‚Äôs strength becomes less than or equal to 000, the enemy dies and its strength becomes 000.

Find the strength of all the enemies after all MMM bullets are fired.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1‚Äã,S2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,SN‚Äã, denoting strength of the enemies.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PMP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_MP1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PM‚Äã, denoting the power of the ithi^{th}ith bullet.
The fourth line contains MMM space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CMC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_MC1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CM‚Äã, denoting the capacity of the ithi^{th}ith bullet.


Output Format
For each test case, output NNN space-separated integers denoting the final strength of the enemies.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains two integers N and M ‚Äì the number of enemies and the number of bullets.
- The second line contains N integers S1 ‚Ä¶ SN ‚Äì the initial strengths of the enemies.
- The third line contains M integers P1 ‚Ä¶ PM ‚Äì the power of each bullet.
- The fourth line contains M integers C1 ‚Ä¶ CM ‚Äì the capacity of each bullet.
All integers are separated by a single space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with N space‚Äëseparated integers ‚Äì the final strengths of the enemies after all bullets have been fired.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Initial strength of the enemies is [51,84,24,19,77,10,34][51, 84, 24, 19, 77, 10, 34][51,84,24,19,77,10,34].

The power of the first bullet is 333 and the capacity is 222. This means that the first bullet reduces the strength of the first and second enemies by 333. The strength of the enemies after the first bullet is fired: [48,81,24,19,77,10,34][48, 81, 24, 19, 77, 10, 34][48,81,24,19,77,10,34].
The power of the second bullet is 666 and the capacity is 222. This means that the second bullet reduces the strength of first and second enemies by 666. The strength of the enemies after the second bullet is fired: [42,75,24,19,77,10,34][42, 75, 24, 19, 77, 10, 34][42,75,24,19,77,10,34].
The strength of enemies after third bullet is fired: [39,72,21,16,74,7,34][39, 72, 21, 16, 74, 7, 34][39,72,21,16,74,7,34].
The strength of enemies after fourth bullet is fired: [29,62,21,16,74,7,34][29, 62, 21, 16, 74, 7, 34][29,62,21,16,74,7,34].

Test case 222: Initial strength of the enemies is [10,5,4,8][10, 5, 4, 8][10,5,4,8].

The power of the first bullet is 444 and the capacity is 222. This means that the first bullet reduces the strength of the first and second enemies by 444. The strength of the enemies after the first bullet is fired: [6,1,4,8][6, 1, 4, 8][6,1,4,8].
The power of the second bullet is 222 and the capacity is 444. This means that the second bullet reduces the strength of the first four enemies by 222. The strength of the enemies after the second bullet is fired: [4,0,2,6][4, 0, 2, 6][4,0,2,6]. Note that due to this bullet, the second enemy dies and its strength becomes 000.
The power of the third bullet is 333 and the capacity is 222. This means that the third bullet reduces the strength of the first two alive enemies by 333. The strength of the enemies after the third bullet is fired: [1,0,0,6][1, 0, 0, 6][1,0,0,6]. Note that since the second enemy is already dead, enemies 111 and 333 are affected by this bullet.

solution.approach:
We have to apply M bullets. The i‚Äëth bullet with power Pi and capacity Ci reduces the strength of the first Ci *alive* enemies by Pi. An enemy dies when its strength becomes ‚â§ 0 and thereafter it is ignored for future capacities.

To support "first k alive" queries we keep a Fenwick tree (Binary Indexed Tree) that stores for each position whether the enemy is still alive (1) or dead (0). The prefix sum gives the number of alive enemies up to any index, and we can binary‚Äësearch on the BIT to find the smallest index pos such that the number of alive enemies in [1, pos] equals Ci. If the total number of alive enemies is smaller than Ci, we simply take pos = N.

All strength modifications are performed on a segment tree that supports range addition (actually subtraction) with lazy propagation and can query the minimum value in a range. After we have pos, we apply a lazy subtraction of Pi on the interval [1, pos]. Some enemies inside this interval may now have strength ‚â§ 0. While the minimum in the whole array is ‚â§ 0 we locate the exact position of a dead enemy (by descending the segment tree), set its strength to 0, and update the BIT to mark it dead (add -1 at that index). Each enemy dies at most once, therefore the total number of such deletions over the whole test case is O(N).

Overall complexity per test case: each bullet performs one BIT search (O(log N)), one segment‚Äëtree range update (O(log N)) and possibly several deletions, but each deletion costs O(log N) and there are at most N deletions. Hence the total time is O((N+M)¬∑log N). The segment tree stores two arrays of size 4¬∑N, the BIT stores N+1 integers ‚Äì overall O(N) memory.

The same idea is implemented in all five languages.
solution.timeComplexity: O((N + M) ¬∑ log N) per test case
